Control Arm.evidence: The comparison randomised patients 1:1 to SOC-alone with or without daily abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg (SOC + AAP), continued until disease progression.
Control Arm.plan_extraction_plan: The control arm is referred to as "standard-of-care (SOC)" throughout the text. Extract "standard-of-care (SOC)" or "SOC-alone".

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048).
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: Page 7, Text: "Reported use of docetaxel within 1 year after first progression was higher among patients allocated to SOC + AAP (32% SOC-alone and 40% SOC + AAP, Chi-square P = .048)." Extract "40%" as the percentage of participants in the SOC + AAP (treatment) arm who received docetaxel within 1 year of first progression. The number of participants is not explicitly stated but can be derived from other information in the document.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.plan_extraction_plan: The text states "94% newly-diagnosed". This implies that 94% of patients had synchronous metastases. Therefore, for the SOC + AAP treatment arm, it can be inferred that 466 patients (93%) had synchronous metastases (Table 1, page 5).

The text states "94% newly-diagnosed". This implies that 94% of patients had synchronous metastases. Therefore, for the SOC-alone control arm, it can be inferred that 475 patients (95%) had synchronous metastases (Table 1, page 5).

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.plan_extraction_plan: The text states "Relapsing 27 (5%)" for the SOC-alone group and "Relapsing 35 (7%)" for the SOC + AAP group. This implies that 5% and 7% of patients had metachronous metastases. Therefore, for the SOC + AAP treatment arm, 35 patients (7%) had metachronous metastases (Table 1, page 5).

The text states "Relapsing 27 (5%)" for the SOC-alone group and "Relapsing 35 (7%)" for the SOC + AAP group. This implies that 5% and 7% of patients had metachronous metastases. Therefore, for the SOC-alone control arm, 27 patients (5%) had metachronous metastases (Table 1, page 5).

This indicates that the study includes patients with synchronous metastases (newly diagnosed with metastatic disease). Therefore, the answer is Yes.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: 94% newly-diagnosed; metastatic disease risk group: 48% high, 44% low, 8% unassessable.

The remaining 6% are relapsing, which indicates that the study includes patients with metachronous metastases. Therefore, the answer is Yes.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: 94% newly-diagnosed; metastatic disease risk group: 48% high, 44% low, 8% unassessable.

Treatment Arm - N.value: Treatment Arm: SOC-alone group, N = 502; Treatment Arm: SOC + AAP group, N = 501
Treatment Arm - N.evidence: Between 15 November 2011 and 17 January 2014, 1917 patients were randomised to the arms of STAMPEDE constituting the 'abiraterone comparison'. Of these, all 1003 patients with metastatic disease were analysed here: 502 (50%) allocated to standard treatment (SOC-alone group) and 501 (50%) to standard treatment plus abiraterone and prednisolone (SOC + AAP group)
Treatment Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: The text states "Between 15 November 2011 and 17 January 2014, 1917 patients were randomised to the arms of STAMPEDE constituting the 'abiraterone comparison'. Of these, all 1003 patients with metastatic disease were analysed here: 502 (50%) allocated to standard treatment (SOC-alone group) and 501 (50%) to standard treatment plus abiraterone and prednisolone (SOC + AAP group)". Therefore: Treatment Arm: SOC-alone group, N = 502; Treatment Arm: SOC + AAP group, N = 501

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.plan_extraction_plan: The first treatment arm is referred to as 'SOC' or 'SOC-alone', which stands for standard-of-care. This is mentioned in the abstract and throughout the text (e.g., page 3). The standard of care is androgen deprivation therapy (ADT).
